pass_metadata.pass_type: manifest-only planning pass, no filesystem changes executed
pass_metadata.zero_edit_scope: No .py, .json, or .yaml file contents are modified in this pass. No import statements are rewritten. No semantic code edits. Filesystem moves only (git mv), except items with action 'inspect_first' or 'keep', which are not touched at all -- not moved, not edited.
pass_metadata.execution_method: git mv for every 'move' and 'archive' action, preserving history. Destination directories are created (mkdir) before the corresponding git mv, never via delete+recreate.
pass_metadata.execution_status: All 51 originally-listed items executed via git mv on 2026-06-21, plus 1 corrective item (time_audit.py, see findings.time_audit_omission_correction) approved and executed the same day. 52 total git mv operations. concept_taxonomy.yaml, vs_enc_invocation_law.yaml, ontology_core.yaml (all action: keep/inspect_first) and vault_logs/ remain untouched as specified.

findings.time_audit_omission_correction: time_audit.py was flagged as looking broken/incomplete during the original scan but was never given a manifest entry -- an oversight caught after execution, when it was the only file left at avm/ root besides the manifest files themselves. User chose destination avm/spec/ (grouped with the other self-evidently unfinished/WIP scripts, technofeudal_bias_audit.py and cg_scribe.py) over avm/archive/ or leaving it in place. Executed via git mv on 2026-06-21. See the corresponding items entry below.
findings.ctx_grok_proto_import_dependency_report: Searched the full projects_2026 tree for references to ctx_grok_proto / CTXGrokProto. Exactly one Python import exists: synapse_engine.py line 51, `from scripts.ctx_grok_proto import CTXGrokProto`, inside the run_synapse() function. This import targets a top-level `scripts` package that does not exist anywhere in projects_2026 (only avm/ctx_grok_proto.py exists, with no 'scripts' wrapper package). The same function also imports `from avm_ops.scripts.vs_enc_orchestrator import VSEncOrchestrator` on line 52, which is equally non-existent -- both are leftover references to the old C:\Users\digitalscorpyun\projects_2025\avm_ops\scripts\ layout (consistent with the stale DEFAULT_SYNAPSE and path_resolver.py FORGE_ROOT constants pointing at projects_2025). Conclusion: run_synapse() is already non-functional today (ModuleNotFoundError on call) regardless of any reorg. ctx_grok_proto.py has no live, working Python import dependency at its current location. Separately, config/vs_enc_invocation_law.yaml lists 'ctx_grok_proto' as the allowed_agents entry for all five invocation types -- this is a governance-config reference by string name, not a filesystem path, so it is unaffected by where the file physically lives. Per your rule, since no working dependency was found, ctx_grok_proto.py is still being kept with the core cluster this pass (least disruption, consistent with 'if in doubt, keep with core') and flagged as prototype debt rather than archived.
findings.ontology_core_header_mismatch: config/ontology_core.yaml's own header comment reads '# war_council/avm_syndicate/configs/ontology_core.yaml', implying its intended canonical home is under war_council/, not avm/config/. No code references this file by path (confirmed via repo-wide grep), so nothing breaks today regardless of its actual location. Per your instruction, marked inspect_first: not moved this pass, flagged for a later Vault-governance decision on whether avm/config/ or a war_council path is correct.
findings.synapse_engine_path_bootstrap_side_effec: synapse_engine.py's _bootstrap_path() computes project_root as the parent of the parent of its own file (os.path.dirname(__file__) + '/..'). Today that resolves to projects_2026/. After the proposed move to avm/core/synapse_engine.py, the same unmodified code will resolve project_root to avm/ instead. This is a pure side-effect of relocation, not a code edit. It does not change functional behavior in practice because the 'scripts' and 'avm_ops.scripts' packages it tries to add to sys.path do not exist at either location -- the function is broken before and after the move. Flagged for awareness, not treated as a blocker.
